extract: Ho-Oh (Japanese: ホウオウ Houou) is a dual-type Fire/Flying Legendary Pokémon introduced in Generation II. It is not known to evolve into or from any other Pokémon. Ho-Oh was the first Generation II Pokémon to be revealed, having been shown in the August 1996 issue of CoroCoro, less than five months after the release of Pokémon Red and Green, and more than three years before the release of Pokémon Gold and Silver. Along with Lugia, it is considered part of the tower duo by fans. It is often viewed as the leader of the legendary beasts. In the movie I Choose You!, it is associated with Marshadow. Ho-Oh is the game mascot of Pokémon Gold Version and its remake, Pokémon HeartGold Version, appearing on the boxart of both games.
